displayName: Regulatory Report (SAR / OFAC block — the inverted notice)
description: The substantive, regulator-facing notice leg of a KYC adverse action, and the inversion of credit ECOA. Where a credit decline notifies the subject with reasons, a KYC adverse action notifies the regulator (SAR to FinCEN, blocking report to OFAC) and is confidential by law from the subject. Notarize-only, and the recipe's Notify subscriptions deliver this type to a regulator endpoint, never to the customer. tippingOffProhibited is conditional-required-true for a SAR (31 U.S.C. 5318(g)(2) makes informing the SAR subject a federal crime). It records that the report was made, on what basis, and to whom: a signed record routed to the regulator and withheld from the subject.

decisionRef.description: recordId of the keystone-onboarding-decision-v1 that triggered the report.

reportType.description: SAR = Suspicious Activity Report to FinCEN | OFAC-block = blocking report on a confirmed SDN match.

filingReference.description: The regulator's filing acknowledgment id where available.

tippingOffProhibited.description: Conditional-required-true for a SAR — affirms the no-tipping-off bar (31 U.S.C. 5318(g)(2)).

basisSummary.description: Internal basis for the report (the 'why', for the examiner — not for the subject).
